title: Internal and External Descriptions of a System
summary: This page discusses the distinction between external and internal system descriptions using a balanced bridge circuit example with a capacitor. It demonstrates that for zero initial conditions, the capacitor is undetectable from external measurements, as the circuit behaves equivalently to a simpler resistive network with y(t) = (2/5)x(t). The page concludes that while external and internal descriptions are often equivalent, internal descriptions can reveal signals invisible to external observation.

figures.caption: A system that cannot be described by external measurements.
